gaps.description: Dynamic dispatch in loops — a loop that evaluates which step to run each iteration based on current state. Any state machine workflow needs this: legal review routing on document classification, data pipelines branching on quality scores. The general primitive: a loop with a dispatch table of steps guarded by when: conditions evaluated per iteration.

Expression functions — ${{ }} expressions that call functions, not just access properties. ${{ loadFile(path) }} is the general case. The function system is domain-agnostic — specific functions are user-provided or workflow-provided. Needed for context loaders, file operations, data derivation.
Timeout actions — configurable responses beyond kill. Steering messages, idle detection, inspect-and-retry. Any long-running agent in any domain needs this.
Budget enforcement — cost ceiling with configurable action (pause, fail, warn). addUsage() accumulates but no gate. Any production workflow needs this.
gaps.details: Accumulation via addUsage() works. No ceiling enforcement yet. Needs design for configurable actions.

gaps.description: Command steps — shell command execution with captured stdout as JSON or text.

A verify block on the step spec declares assertions the runtime checks after the step completes. Not a separate step — built into the step definition. Assertion types: file exists, file changed, output field non-empty, output field matches pattern, shell command exits 0. These are deterministic, no LLM.

Requirement tracking (must-haves) — the step's input declares requirements (via schema or explicit requires field). The verify block checks the output addresses them. Cross-references input requirements against output content using heuristic matching (keyword/token presence in output fields). Domain-agnostic: works for code tasks, compliance points, synthesis criteria.

Graduated failure — not binary pass/fail. The failure cascade: soft timeout → steer the agent → hard timeout → inspect durability (what artifacts exist so far?) → retry with gap analysis injected → skip with blocker placeholder so the pipeline advances. Configurable per step: how many retries, what steering message, whether to skip or halt.

Blocker propagation — when a step is skipped after exhausting retries, a blocker placeholder artifact is written. Downstream steps see the placeholder and can decide what to do. The pipeline doesn't silently drop work — it records what failed and why, in a structured artifact visible to subsequent steps and to the user.

Pre-step validation — gate checks on inputs before dispatch. Already supported as gate steps. The richer form: validate that the previous step's output has the content quality needed for this step to succeed. Not just 'does the file exist' but 'does the plan have a verification section, are scope estimates within thresholds.'
Expression parser: adjacent ${{ }}${{ }} in a single string — whole-value regex can misparse them. Pre-existing limitation.
Delete old .md demo agents — .agent.yaml versions are the source of truth, .md files are dead weight.
Remove dead writeStepOutput in state.ts — superseded by output.ts persistStepOutput().

No 'do phase' workflow exists. Main agent saying 'do phase 8' requires: load phase spec by number, plan decomposition across all specs, forEach implement, route results to blocks, verify against phase success criteria, update phase status. self-implement.workflow.yaml is close but needs a load-phase command step at the front and phase-status-update at the end.
gaps.details: Blocked by: nested workflow invocation (Phase 6) for per-spec cycles. Workaround: flatten — one planning step reads full phase spec, produces flat plans array, forEach implements all, one verify pass. Add load-phase and update-phase-status command steps to self-implement.workflow.yaml.

gaps.description: forEach step body can only be a single step type (agent, transform, command, gate). Cannot invoke a sub-workflow per iteration. Needed for: per-spec plan-implement-verify cycles within a phase. This is the 'workflow' step type placeholder from Phase 6.
gaps.details: Phase 6 deliverable. Without it, phase execution must flatten all specs into one plan-implement-verify pass rather than modular per-spec cycles.

gaps.description: No tool or command exists for agents or main context to record gaps during execution. Gap recording is post-hoc and opt-in. Need a record-gap tool available mid-task that writes to gaps.json — as frictionless as git commit.
gaps.details: Monitor writes directly to gaps.json with source:'monitor'. No agent steering needed — monitor observes friction in execution trace, appends gap, done. Human/main-context reviews monitor-created gaps and marks actionable or deletes. Query by source: gaps.filter(g => g.source === 'monitor'). Three-layer design: (1) monitor writes gaps directly (non-bypassable), (2) execution-results.issues[] required in schema (agent-reported), (3) route-results routes agent-reported issues with source:'agent'.

gaps.description: No safe mechanism to pass structured data to command steps. Shell argument quoting breaks on JSON with quotes. Current workaround: persist to known file path via output.path, next step reads from disk. Need: stdin piping, env var injection, or temp file abstraction in the command step runtime.
gaps.details: Discovered when fix-audit workflow failed: ${{ expr | json }} in shell arguments breaks on JSON containing single quotes. All command steps rewritten to read files directly. The output.path → read-from-disk pattern works but is manual and requires coordinating paths between steps.

gaps.description: Finding p1-type-any-typebox was already resolved: src/index.ts correctly imports Type from @sinclair/typebox with no `let Type: any` present.
